title: SafeRE shows what agent-assisted software engineering looks like in practice
friendlyTitle: A developer built a serious Java library with AI agents
summary: Former Google engineer Eddie Aftandilian described building SafeRE, a production-quality linear-time Java regular expression library, with agents writing the code while he directed architecture, review, testing, and corrections.
friendlySummary: Eddie Aftandilian wrote about using AI agents to help build SafeRE, a Java regex library designed to avoid slow and risky regex behavior.
whyItMatters: The post is useful because it avoids magic-demo framing: agents helped tackle a substantial project, but only with human decomposition, review, and a serious correctness strategy. That is close to how durable agentic engineering is likely to work.
friendlyWhyItMatters: It shows that AI coding agents can be powerful, but they still need a human to plan, test, review, and fix mistakes.
